w:comments.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Looks like only inference is assumed in this document. Will training (fine-tuning) will be added later? We'll also need GEMMA (JAX).

This was clarified in map day, INC only targets low precision inference recipe. Training recipe is likely from M-LM/TE side. For GEMMA(JAX), it is still aligning inside AIFW team and likely covered in this doc later
Regarding low-precision fine-tuning, the recipes are not yet very clear, so we’d like to focus on inference, as it’s also the main usage for JGS
Does it mean SDPA stays in bf16 for now?

Yes, SDPA is not quantized in current FP8/FP4 models published in HF from vllm/llm-compressor or Nvdia ModelOpt. We do have SDPA FP8 recipe on Gaudi, but it does not scale well on wide models (e.g. qwen2/2.5 series), we suggest keep it consistent with field for now.
so no MXFP8 for CRI?
Some of the models in PO and PRQ for CRI don't have an fp8 recipe but have an MXFP8 recipe, hence the question
updated to MXFP8 to make it consistent

@Wang, Mengni
Owner:
@He, Xin3
in 2.1 it is mentioned that model has MXFP8/4 formats on CRI. Will those be added further to the recipe? Accuracy results are available as well?
This section needs to be moved back to section 2 as it is one of CRI key model now. Also MXFP8/MXFP4 recipe is ready with accuracy result.
@He, Xin3
will update this part after llama3.3 70B model acc updated
Which rounding mode is recommended for weights? Also nVidia (RCEIL)?
For weight, OCP scheme is always used. RCEIL is for the activation only in our [design](https://github.com/intel/auto-round/blob/57d1a8acfbe98ee55330caedbfb47bdc2a83276f/auto_round/schemes.py#L205).

JGS seems to be moving forward with support of nvfp4+ (so e5m3 block scales without extra tensor scaling). Is that something we need to support as well? Does it come as additional recipe or replaces the nvfp4 one?
In our experiments, e5m3 without tensor scaling is slightly better than e4m3 with tensor scaling. Not sure if this support is finalized in JGS, if so, we can replace it.
@Shen, Haihao
, do u know if it is confirmed?

NVFP4+ (element E2M1 and scale E5M3) has been approved in JGS, so it will get prioritized from recipe perspective, while assuming NVFP4 is still required from enabling perspective (INC, oneDNN, FWK) to be compatible with NV’s ecosystem.
could we put the type for activation and weights explicitly instead of W8A8 (to make sure we are exactly aligned and don't misinterpret implicit info)
updated
Should it be UE4M3 (sign bit ignored)?
Yes, it will be more accurate. will use UE4M3/UE5M3
For the extra scale, could you specify its datatype?
updated
Does it include the sdpa part in FP8? If so, what flavor of FP8 - MXFP8, 128x128 or per-tensor?
No, the self-attn here refers to the attention module (Q/K/V proj Linear, SDPA, output proj Linear). We keep this part the same as the DeepSeek released model.
The linear modules use FP8 (128×128 block size), and the SDPA uses BF16.
could we put the type for activation and weights explicitly instead of W8A8 (to make sure we are exactly aligned and don't misinterpret implicit info)
updated
Should it be UE4M3 (sign bit ignored)?
Yes, it will be more accurate. will use UE4M3/UE5M3
For the extra scale, could you specify its datatype?
updated
Using lm_eval 0.4.10
could we put the type for activation and weights explicitly instead of W8A8 (to make sure we are exactly aligned and don't misinterpret implicit info)
updated
Thanks. just to make sure: fp8 is e4m3 (nv recipe), right?
Yes, it is reflected in “Element format” field, will not use different format for weight and activation
Unable to find the MXFP4 reference model for Llama4 Scout, could you please help in pointing to the model location.
Or is it loaded in higher precision and then casted post load weights

Llama seriesl model is not able to upload to HF model hub due to Intel legal feedback. but you can quantize locally with following example in INC repo: https://github.com/intel/neural-compressor/tree/master/examples/pytorch/multimodal-modeling/quantization/auto_round/llama4
If LInear is the only quantized op, is the expectation here that Linear inputs are MXFP4 but output is fp16/bf16? I guess this also means that previous layers to Linear should output MXFP4, right?

If the previous layer is also Linear, then it depends on HW, not sure if MXFP4 GEMM will support output MXFP4 directly though I think it should provide the option. but in FWK level, it always has case that previous layer output is fp16/bf16, in this case, Linear likely needs option to support fp16/bf16 input and conduct dynamic quant to MXFP4
@Huang, Tai
, is MOE included in Linear? Or MOE not quantized?
In general, linear ops in MoE are included, router is excluded normally, and sometime shared_expert is excluded for accuracy reason.
could we put the type for activation and weights explicitly instead of W8A8 (to make sure we are exactly aligned and don't misinterpret implicit info)
updated
Could you add a section before with exact definition of MaxAbs and conversion formulas?

For fp scales I assume the scale is computed in f32 and then converted to scale_dt, but in case of e8m0, it could be more complex (see cublas documentation for one flavor: https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cublas/#d-block-quantization)
Sure, will add a section for definitions, also according to recent direction from Oren, we need to prioritize MXFP8 instead of standard FP8
That is news. Is there any rationale to pursue MXFP8 instead of commonly used fp8 recipes?
IIRC, MXFP8 has low traction since it does not provide better accuracy nor better performance. Do we have new data that prompts us to differ from already implemented/used fp8 recipes?
This is still an open, please refer to
https://confluence.habana-labs.com/display/FS/JGS+AI+Core+stack+SW+Arch+sync

(ww26’25, open a. FP8). Oren is pushing this direction from performance perspective. We are working on accuracy verification on large model like DeepSeek R1. according to previous experience, it is likely ok on large model, but we also raised the risk on small model
Add section 1.3 & 1.4 for scaling method definitions
could we put the type for activation and weights explicitly instead of W8A8 (to make sure we are exactly aligned and don't misinterpret implicit info)
updated
Do you mean UE4M3 (sign bit ignored)?
Updated
Just to make sure: Autoround applies to INC, but from oneDNN perspective, this just implies statically quantized weights, correct?
You are right, AutoRound algo is applied in quantization stage to generate quantized weight. From oneDNN perspective, it is just statically quantized weight.
Just to make sure: Autoround applies to INC, but from oneDNN perspective, this just implies statically quantized weights, correct?
You are right, AutoRound algo is applied in quantization stage to generate quantized weight. From oneDNN perspective, it is just statically quantized weight.
@Lv, Kaokao
, suggest to use upper-case convention for data type in this section so that the whole document is consistent
fixed them
@Lv, Kaokao
can we just try using MXFP8-E4M3 for backward? Or this is what Nvdia recommends.
we can try MXFP8-E4M3 for backward. there are no recommends from nvidia doc
Fine-Tuning here is full parameter or using LoRA? If use LoRA, shall we provide LoRA config?
@Lv, Kaokao
why mxfp8/fp8 needs more gpu memory than bf16?
mxfp8/fp8 need save more extra_state, like fwd/bwd scales. When train the 70B model, set the optimizer cpu offloading to avoid OOM.
